label: The Writ and Tankard
description: The Writ and Tankard occupies a narrow brick ground floor beneath a soot-stained gable, its facade bearing the city’s licensing seal and a slate board of posted ordinances. Inside, salvaged ashlar piers and newer timber beams support a low ceiling blackened by lamp smoke, while small shuttered windows keep the room in perpetual half-light. A long scarred bar and fixed trestle tables enforce orderly circulation, and a railed alcove serves as a docket-board for notices, interdiction placards, and caravan tallies. The air holds the mingled taint of spilled ale, damp wool, and iron from the nearby courts and countinghouses.
